_help: Copy this file to config.json (in this same folder), then fill in connectorKey. Lines starting with _help are just notes - the connector ignores them.
_help_cloudUrl: The web address of your QuoteKaro app. Keep the default unless you run your own copy of the site.
_help_connectorKey: Your private key. Get it from the app: Setup > Tally. It looks like tk_ followed by 40 letters and numbers. Never share it - anyone with this key can read your won quotes.
_help_tallyUrl: Where TallyPrime listens on this PC. 9000 is Tally's default port - only change this if you changed the port inside Tally.
_help_intervalSec: How often to check for new won quotes, in seconds. 60 (one minute) is fine for almost everyone.
_help_voucherType: The Tally voucher type to create for each won quote. Leave as Sales Order unless you made your own voucher type in Tally and want that instead.
_help_salesLedger: The sales ledger each order's amount is booked under. If your Tally company already has one (usually called Sales or Sales Account), put its exact name here; otherwise the connector creates a ledger named Sales under Sales Accounts.
_help_dryRun: Set to true for a safe first test: the connector prints the Tally XML on screen instead of sending it, and saves nothing to the cloud.
_help_pullOutstanding: true = also read customer outstanding balances (Sundry Debtors) from Tally every round and show them in the app. Set to false to turn that off.
